discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with semis leading (SMH +2.6%, AVGO +5%, TSM +5.3%) on SK Hynix record results and AI memory demand, while defense (ITA -1.8%) lags. Mid-cap movers are mostly earnings-driven singles. CALX post-earnings collapse and BBY weakness stand out against the broader rally.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% leading on SK Hynix record profits and AI memory pricing
VIX +4.2% to 19.72 rising despite equity rally — hedging into earnings week
ITA -1.77% defense weakness, possibly on Navy Secretary departure headline
XLF -0.17% and KRE -0.55% lagging the rally — regionals flat

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Post-earnings reaction; likely guidance miss or margin concern given 4x volume and 14% drop on a risk-on tape. No specific news in feed.
discoveries.catalyst: Presumed Q1 earnings release — need to confirm specifics
research_pointers: Check Q1 print: revenue vs consensus, gross margin trajectory, BEAD-related order commentary
Listen to call for guidance on Tier-2/Tier-3 carrier spending cycle
Compare reaction vs peers ADTN, CIEN, EXTR (EXTR also down 5.8% today — possible sector read-through)
Short interest and options positioning into print
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Guidance was actually in-line or raised, and the drop was sympathy/technical — would argue overdone. Conversely, a material full-year cut would validate the move.

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Possible sympathy selling with CALX on networking/carrier equipment weakness, or own earnings/pre-announcement. Move is outsized vs a +1.67% QQQ day.
discoveries.catalyst: Unclear — check for earnings date proximity or read-through from CALX
research_pointers: Confirm whether EXTR has reported or is pre-announcing
If purely sympathy to CALX (different end-markets: enterprise vs broadband access), could be overdone
Check CSCO, ANET, JNPR relative moves to isolate signal
discoveries.what_would_falsify: EXTR issued its own negative pre-announcement — then move is rational, not sympathy.

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Earnings-driven; 5-day +10.9% suggests the move is building on a print or raised guide. Supply-chain software has been a resilient category.
research_pointers: Confirm earnings details: cloud RPO growth, services revenue outlook
Management tone on enterprise IT budgets
Valuation check — MANH typically trades rich; verify multiple vs historical
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Move is not earnings-driven but rumor/M&A speculation, which would be less durable.

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: Diverging from risk-on tape and from GME (+4.9%). Could be tariff/consumer concern tied to Trump approval rating news and 159B tariff refund rhetoric, or a specific downgrade/data point on consumer electronics.
discoveries.catalyst: Possible analyst action or consumer-sentiment read-through; Trump economic approval at low of term headline could pressure discretionary
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrades or channel checks on TV/PC demand
Cross-reference with credit card data releases on electronics category
Examine whether tariff court decision refund chatter has specific BBY implications
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A specific negative catalyst (guidance cut, downgrade) appears — then the move is rational on news we haven't captured.

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Against XBI +0.67%, RGEN -6.25% is a clear idiosyncratic move. Volume only 1.2x avg suggests not capitulation. Bioprocessing peer weakness or company-specific news.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — check for pre-announcement, customer concentration news, or peer read-through (DHR, SRT, Sartorius)
research_pointers: Earnings calendar confirmation
Sartorius/Danaher bioprocessing commentary this week
China bioprocessing demand headlines
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A pre-announcement or guide cut — then rational. Absence of news with continued drift would suggest overdone.

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments
discoveries.mechanism: Semi cap-eq riding SK Hynix HBM demand narrative and broader SMH +2.6% move. 5-day +18% confirms thematic flow.
discoveries.catalyst: SK Hynix record results, AI memory pricing, AVGO/TSM strength
research_pointers: Veeco's HBM/advanced packaging exposure (laser annealing, MBE)
Order book commentary from peers LRCX, AMAT, KLAC this earnings cycle
Whether rally has extended past fundamental exposure
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Veeco's actual HBM exposure is smaller than market is pricing — then it's sympathy-overextension.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix record Q1 profit on AI memory demand + TSM/AVGO strength

secondary_opportunities.relationship: supplier (semi cap equipment to memory fabs)

catalyst_chains.primary_event: CALX large post-earnings drop; potential carrier/broadband equipment read-through

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy (different end-market but networking hardware grouping)

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump attacks Supreme Court over $159B tariff refund decision and birthright citizenship case
trump_signals.note: The $159B tariff refund ruling is material for importers of consumer electronics, apparel, and durable goods. Tone suggests administration may seek workarounds (new tariff authorities, executive actions). Monitor importers for headline risk. Does not change the refund itself but raises uncertainty on forward tariff regime.

discovery.no_signals_note: Most mid-cap movers appear earnings-driven with limited public news in feed — confidence is capped at 2-3 pending earnings confirmation. Michael should verify earnings calendar before acting on CALX/EXTR/MANH/RGEN reads.

ai_announcements.headline: Google Workspace/Chrome/Gmail get deep Gemini AI integration; new TPU chips challenge Nvidia
ai_announcements.summary: Google launched Workspace Intelligence (AI intern functions), Chrome auto-browse for enterprise, Gmail AI Overviews, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, AI in Maps, and two new TPUs positioned as faster/cheaper than prior gen.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan's supply chain/WMS software is domain-specific; likely reporting earnings or benefiting from agent-platform narrative rather than Google Workspace news. Move appears earnings-driven, not AI-news driven.
research_pointers: Check MANH earnings release this week
Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform positioning vs MANH agentic commerce

ai_announcements.headline: Google's new TPUs position as Nvidia alternative, but Google still embracing Nvidia; Thinking Machines signs multibillion deal on GB300s
ai_announcements.summary: Mixed signal for semi equipment: continued hyperscaler capex on leading-edge chips.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Semi equipment benefits from both TPU and Nvidia production ramps; AI capex tailwind regardless of which chip wins.
research_pointers: VECO exposure to advanced packaging / HBM / laser annealing for AI chips
Whether move is earnings-related

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most of today's big movers (CALX -14%, EXTR -5.8%, RGEN -6.2%, BBY -4.6%, GME +4.9%, WEX +4.2%) do not have a clear mechanistic link to the AI announcements in the news set. CALX and EXTR drops on high volume look like earnings/guidance events in networking, not AI displacement. RGEN and BBY moves also likely idiosyncratic. No Anthropic announcements in this batch. Recommend not forcing AI narratives onto these moves without earnings confirmation.
